label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out if following these instructions will bring me back to the starting point. Let me break down each step carefully. First, I need to visualize my starting position. Let's assume I'm facing north at the beginning. The instructions are: Turn left. Turn left. Take 10 steps. Take 6 steps. Turn left. Take 3 steps. Take 3 steps. Starting off, when I turn left from facing north, I'll be facing west. Turning left again, another 90 degrees, so now I'm facing south. Now, take 10 steps south. Then take 6 steps in the same direction, which is still south. So from the starting point, I've moved 10 + 6 = 16 steps south. Next instruction is to turn left. If I'm facing south and turn left, I'll be facing east. Then take 3 steps east. Then another 3 steps east. So that's a total of 6 steps east. Now, let's plot this out. From the starting point (let's say coordinates (0,0)), moving 16 steps south would take me to (0, -16). Then moving 6 steps east would take me to (6, -16). So, the final position is (6, -16). That's 6 units east and 16 units south from the starting point. That's not the same as the starting point (0,0), so I haven't returned. Wait, but maybe I made an assumption about the starting direction that might not be necessary? Let me check again. The problem doesn't specify the initial direction, so I assumed north, but maybe the actual direction doesn't matter because the relative movements would be the same regardless of the starting direction? Let's test that. Suppose I start facing east. First left turn would face me north, another left turn west. Then 10 steps west, 6 steps west (total 16 west). Turn left (south) and take 3 steps south, then 3 more steps south (total 6 south). Final position would be 16 west and 6 south from start. Coordinates (-16, -6). Not back to start. If I start facing south. First left turn west, then left turn north. 10 steps north, 6 steps north (16 north). Turn left (west) and take 3 steps west, then 3 more west. Final position: 16 north and 6 west. Coordinates (-6, 16). Still not starting point. If I start facing west. First left turn south, then left turn east. 10 steps east, 6 steps east (16 east). Turn left (north) and take 3 steps north, then 3 more north. Final position: 16 east and 6 north. Coordinates (16, 6). Again, not back to start. So regardless of the starting direction, the final
